title: Seven Splashes Deep
synopsis: A small bottlenose dolphin calf's invention creates unexpected splashes in the school gymnasium, as told by the old scoreboard who watches seven mysterious gifts arrive throughout the week, each revealing something new about making, building, and the joy of things not going quite as planned.

pages.text:
The gymnasium lights flicker on one hour before the big water polo game, and I—the old scoreboard hanging high above—watch everything unfold below. I've been here for thirty-seven years, you know, counting points and keeping time, but I've never seen anything quite like this week.

Down on the gymnasium floor, Blue—a small bottlenose dolphin calf with pale silver-blue skin, a curving smile, bright dark eyes, and a single pale scar on her dorsal fin—discovers a tiny wrapped package by the equipment closet.

'What's this?' she whistles softly, her flippers turning the package over and over. Inside the silver paper sits a single gear, no bigger than a sand dollar, gleaming brass and perfectly round.

Blue's invention sits nearby—a curious contraption of tubes and springs she's been working on all semester for the school science fair. It's meant to make the perfect water fountain for after games, she told her teacher last week. Something to help everyone feel refreshed.

She holds up the gear, tilting her head. 'You look important,' she says to it, and clicks it into place on her machine. The gear fits perfectly, as if it belonged there all along.

illustration.description: Blue, a small bottlenose dolphin calf with pale silver-blue skin, a curving smile, bright dark eyes, and a single pale scar on her dorsal fin, sits in a gymnasium with her family around a warm wooden table, examining a brass gear. The scene has warm yellows and oranges, with kitchen-like coziness despite the gym setting, steam rising from nearby equipment, and flour-like dust motes in the air.
illustration.alt: Blue examining a brass gear in a cozy gymnasium setting

pages.text:
Tuesday afternoon brings another gift—this time tucked inside Blue's locker. A small spring, silver and tight-coiled, with a note that simply says 'For when you need a bounce.'

Blue's older brother Finn swims over as she examines the spring. 'Still working on that fountain?' he asks, his own pale blue skin catching the light.

'It's almost ready,' Blue says, adding the spring to her invention. 'I just need to figure out the water pressure. It should make gentle streams, perfect for sipping.'

Finn nods encouragingly, though I notice him glance at the contraption with something that might be concern. The machine is growing more complex by the day—gears and springs and tubes all connected in ways that would puzzle an engineer.

Blue tests a small lever, and a tiny puff of air whistles out. 'Perfect!' she clicks happily. 'Tomorrow I'll add water and see how it works.'

From up here, I can see what she cannot—how all those springs and gears seem to be building pressure, storing energy like a held breath. But Blue works with the patient joy of someone who believes in what she's making.

illustration.description: Blue, a small bottlenose dolphin calf with pale silver-blue skin, a curving smile, bright dark eyes, and a single pale scar on her dorsal fin, works at a warm kitchen table with her brother Finn, both examining a silver spring. The scene glows with warm yellows and oranges, steam rising from their shared meal, flour dusting the wooden table surface as the family gathers around.
illustration.alt: Blue and her brother Finn examining a spring at a kitchen table

pages.text:
Wednesday's gift arrives in Blue's backpack—a small copper tube, hollow and smooth. 'Another piece,' she murmurs, and I watch her eyes light up with understanding.

Her mother, Marina, glides over during lunch break. 'How's the project coming, little splash?' she asks warmly.

'I think I understand now,' Blue says, connecting the tube to her machine. 'Each piece has been exactly what I needed. The gear for timing, the spring for pressure, and now this tube for directing the flow.'

Marina examines the invention thoughtfully. 'It's quite impressive,' she says. 'Very... energetic-looking.'

Blue beams with pride. 'Wait until you see it work! I'm going to test it right before the game tomorrow when everyone's here.'

I've seen many inventions in this gymnasium over the years—science fair projects, art installations, mechanical demonstrations. Most work exactly as intended. But this one... this one hums with possibility, with the unpredictable magic that happens when young minds build something entirely their own.

The copper tube gleams in the afternoon light, and Blue adjusts it carefully, making sure the angle is just right.

illustration.description: Blue, a small bottlenose dolphin calf with pale silver-blue skin, a curving smile, bright dark eyes, and a single pale scar on her dorsal fin, sits with her mother Marina at a cozy kitchen table with warm yellow and orange lighting, connecting a copper tube to her invention while steam rises from nearby pots and flour dusts the homey atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Blue and her mother Marina working with a copper tube in a warm kitchen setting

pages.text:
Thursday brings not one gift, but two—a small valve and a pressure gauge, wrapped together with a ribbon that shimmers like seaweed. Blue finds them waiting on the gymnasium bleachers when she arrives early for practice.

'Someone knows exactly what I'm building,' she whispers to herself, and I have to agree. Each gift has been perfectly chosen, as if the giver understands her invention better than she does herself.

Blue's father, Ray, helps her install the new pieces. 'This gauge will tell you how much pressure is building up,' he explains, his deep voice echoing slightly in the empty gymnasium.

'And the valve?'

'That's for release,' Ray says. 'Every good fountain needs a way to control its flow.'

But as Blue connects the valve, I notice she installs it backwards—the release will actually increase the pressure instead of reducing it. Ray doesn't notice, and Blue is too excited to double-check.

'Tomorrow's the big day,' Blue says, patting her invention fondly. 'Everyone will love having fresh fountain water after the game.'

The pressure gauge needle already quivers slightly, though no water has been added yet.

illustration.description: Blue, a small bottlenose dolphin calf with pale silver-blue skin, a curving smile, bright dark eyes, and a single pale scar on her dorsal fin, works alongside her father Ray at a warm kitchen table, installing a valve and pressure gauge on her invention. The scene is bathed in warm yellows and oranges with steam rising from cooking pots and flour creating a homey, family atmosphere around their wooden dining table.
illustration.alt: Blue and her father Ray installing mechanical parts in a cozy kitchen setting

pages.text:
Friday's gifts arrive all at once—five small items in a single box: a timer, a switch, a hose, a nozzle, and a small card that reads 'For the most wonderful splash.'

Blue works feverishly to attach everything before the game begins. The gymnasium fills with families and friends, all chattering excitedly about the match ahead. But Blue only has eyes for her invention.

'Ready for the demonstration?' asks Mrs. Coral, the science teacher.

Blue nods proudly. 'It's going to be perfect. Everyone can have a nice drink of fountain water after the game.'

She fills the machine's reservoir with water, flips the switch, and steps back. The gears begin to turn, the springs compress, and the pressure gauge needle climbs higher and higher.

'Um, Blue...' Finn starts to say, but it's too late.

With a tremendous WHOOSH, Blue's invention works—just not the way she intended. Instead of a gentle drinking fountain, it creates the most magnificent water show anyone has ever seen. Streams arc and dance through the air, creating rainbows in the gymnasium lights, splashing and sparkling in patterns that make everyone gasp with delight.

Blue stares in amazement. 'It's not what I planned,' she says.

'No,' laughs Marina, 'it's even better.'

And from my perch high above, I have to agree—sometimes the most wonderful things happen when our inventions surprise us.

illustration.description: Blue, a small bottlenose dolphin calf with pale silver-blue skin, a curving smile, bright dark eyes, and a single pale scar on her dorsal fin, stands in a warm, kitchen-like gymnasium with her whole family gathered around a wooden table, watching in amazement as her invention creates beautiful water arcs and rainbows. The scene glows with warm yellows and oranges, steam and water droplets filling the air like flour dust, creating a cozy family moment of shared wonder.
illustration.alt: Blue and her family watching her invention create a beautiful water display

metadata.educationalValue: Encourages creativity, problem-solving, and embracing unexpected outcomes

coverPrompt.description: Blue, a small bottlenose dolphin calf with pale silver-blue skin, a curving smile, bright dark eyes, and a single pale scar on her dorsal fin, stands in a cozy kitchen with warm yellow and orange light, holding a small mechanical contraption made of gears and springs, while steam rises from pots on the stove and flour dusts the countertops where her family gathers around a wooden table.
coverPrompt.alt: A dolphin calf in a warm kitchen holding an invention

questions.question: Who tells this story?
questions.explanation: The story is told by the old scoreboard that hangs in the gymnasium and watches everything happen.

questions.question: What was Blue trying to build?
questions.explanation: Blue wanted to make a drinking fountain for after games, so everyone could have refreshing water.

questions.question: How many gifts did Blue receive during the week?
questions.explanation: Blue received seven gifts total: a gear, spring, tube, valve, pressure gauge, and then five final items in one box.

questions.question: What happened when Blue tested her invention?
choices: It made a beautiful water show
questions.explanation: Instead of making a simple fountain, Blue's invention created magnificent water streams and rainbows that amazed everyone.

questions.question: How did Blue feel about her invention not working as planned?
questions.explanation: Blue was amazed by the beautiful result, and her family thought it was even better than what she had originally planned.
